name: Quick Christmas Tree Bread
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup (4 ounces) diced dried fruit mix
1 1/2 teaspoons pumpkin pie spice or ground cinnamon, or
8 tablespoons (1 stick) cold, unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
Granulated sugar or homemade vanilla pearl
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
2. Mix milk, granulated sugar, and egg yolk in a bowl until well combined.
3. Stir in the diced dried fruit. Let it stand until ready to use.
4. In a large bowl, combine flour, baking powder, spice (cinnamon and cloves), and salt. Add cold butter and mix with your hands or a pastry blender until the mixture resembles fine granules.
5. Stir in the milk mixture and pour over the dry ingredients. Mix well to form a soft dough.
6. Turn out the dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead 10 times (or until no longer sticky; if needed, add a little more flour).
7. Cut off about one-fourth of the dough. Shape the remaining dough into an ungreased rectangular shape on a sheet at least 16 inches long.
8. Using both hands, roll the dough into a flat triangle measuring approximately 12 inches long and 9 inches wide across the bottom.
9. With scissors, make ten diagonal cuts down each long side of the triangle, stopping about 1 inch from the center.
10. Form a small piece of reserved dough into a trunk at the base of the tree and the remaining dough into a "pot."
11. Refrigerate the shaped bread for up to 2 weeks or freeze for later use.
12. Bake the tree for 10 minutes, then beat egg white until frothy. Brush over the hot bread and sprinkle with sugar.
13. Continue baking for 5 to 8 more minutes, or until lightly browned. Transfer the bread to a wire rack and cool at least 2 hours before serving. Guests can break off pieces as desired.
Yield: 10 to 12 small servings
